main.description: Measurement data related to a hypercert record (e.g. an activity and its impact).

subjects.description: References to the subject(s) the measurement is connected to—this may be an activity claim, outcome claim, evaluation, or even another measurement. This is optional as the measurement can exist before the claim is recorded.

measurers.description: DIDs of the entity (or entities) that measured this data

metric.description: The metric being measured, e.g. forest area restored, number of users, etc.

unit.description: The unit of the measured value (e.g. kg CO₂e, hectares, %, index score).

startDate.description: The time when the measurement was started.

endDate.description: The time when the measurement ended. If it was a one time measurement, the endDate should be equal to the startDate.

measurementMethodType.description: Short identifier for the measurement methodology

measurementMethodURI.description: URI pointing to detailed methodology documentation, a standard protocol, or a measurement procedure.

evidenceURI.description: URIs to related evidence or underlying data (e.g. org.hypercerts.claim.evidence records or raw datasets)

location.description: A strong reference to the location where the measurement was taken. The record referenced must conform with the lexicon app.certified.location

locations.description: Optional geographic references related to where the measurement was taken. Each referenced record must conform with the app.certified.location lexicon.

createdAt.description: Client-declared timestamp when this record was originally created
